skill.name: bazel-build-optimization
skill.description: Optimize Bazel builds for large-scale monorepos. Use when configuring Bazel, implementing remote execution, or optimizing build performance for enterprise codebases.
skill.summary: Configure, profile, and optimize Bazel builds for large monorepos.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command, network, filesystem, crypto, and obfuscation patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are documentation examples, Bazel templates, placeholder service URLs, checksum fields, and local cache paths, with no evidence of malicious execution or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Require User Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes Bazel, Git, sed, tr, dot, and wc command examples. These are relevant build-analysis commands, but users should inspect targets and repository state before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples for Bazel workflows, not hidden code. They can still affect local files or consume resources when a user chooses to execute them.

low_findings.title: Remote Build Service Placeholders
low_findings.description: The skill shows remote cache, remote execution, and build event service endpoints using example hostnames. This is normal Bazel configuration guidance, but users must replace placeholders with trusted internal services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoints are example domains in a .bazelrc template and are labeled as optional remote caching or execution settings. I found no evidence that the skill sends data to these hosts by itself.

low_findings.title: Local Home Cache Paths
low_findings.description: The skill recommends Bazel disk and repository cache paths under the user's home cache directory. This is a normal local performance pattern, not credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths point to Bazel cache directories and do not reference secrets or credential files. The static hidden-file warning is a false positive in this context.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto Signals Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged crypto-related terms, but the reviewed content uses sha256 as dependency integrity metadata and mnemonic as a Bazel action label. No private key or seed handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain table text, dependency checksum fields, and a Bazel mnemonic field. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic use or secret material processing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Example
dangerous_patterns.description: A Bazel query example uses shell command substitution to derive changed files from git output. This is a legitimate workflow pattern, but it should be adapted carefully for repositories with unusual file names.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is present in a documented example and has a clear build-analysis purpose. The risk is limited because the skill does not execute it automatically.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bazel Action Execution Template
dangerous_patterns.description: A custom rule template uses ctx.actions.run to invoke a repository-defined builder. This is standard Bazel rule authoring, but users should ensure the builder target is trusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The execution call is part of an explicit custom-rule example with structured arguments. It is not obfuscated or connected to external data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Large Bazel workspaces can suffer from slow builds, weak caching, and unclear dependency graphs. This skill provides practical templates for remote caching, execution, profiling, and BUILD file design.
actual_capabilities: Creates starter WORKSPACE.bazel patterns for JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python rules.
Provides .bazelrc settings for local resources, disk caches, remote cache, remote execution, CI, tests, and coverage.
Shows BUILD.bazel examples for TypeScript libraries, Python libraries, tests, and binaries.
Explains a custom Bazel rule structure for building Docker image artifacts.
Lists Bazel query commands for dependencies, reverse dependencies, test discovery, and graph generation.
Gives profiling commands for build timing, execution logs, memory profiles, and analysis cache behavior.
limitations: Does not inspect a repository automatically or calculate measured build bottlenecks.
Uses placeholder remote service URLs that must be replaced with trusted infrastructure.
Does not provide complete enterprise authentication or secrets management setup.
Requires users to adapt versions, rule sets, and labels to their workspace.

use_cases.title: Plan Bazel Adoption
use_cases.description: Create a starting workspace structure, rule set, and BUILD file approach for a growing monorepo.

use_cases.title: Improve CI Build Time
use_cases.description: Review cache, resource, test, and remote execution settings before tuning a Bazel-based CI pipeline.

use_cases.title: Debug Dependency Graphs
use_cases.description: Use Bazel query patterns to find dependencies, reverse dependencies, tests, and graph size issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Bazel Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new monorepo
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this Bazel build optimization skill to propose a basic WORKSPACE.bazel, .bazelrc, and package layout for my monorepo. My main languages are [languages].

prompt_templates.title: Tune Local Build Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current .bazelrc settings and suggest local cache, CPU, RAM, and test-output improvements. Explain each change and any tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Remote Cache and CI Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare shared caching for continuous integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design Bazel remote cache and CI settings for [repository size] with [CI system]. Include safe placeholder endpoints and required validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Advanced Build Graph Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these Bazel profile and query results, identify likely causes of slow builds and propose targeted BUILD file, visibility, dependency, and remote execution changes.

output_examples.input: Ask for a starter Bazel configuration for a TypeScript and Python monorepo.
output: A workspace plan with JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python rule dependencies.
A .bazelrc outline for local resources, disk cache, test output, and coverage.
Notes about replacing placeholder versions and verifying dependency checksums.

output_examples.input: Ask how to diagnose slow Bazel builds in CI.
output: A profiling sequence using build profiles, execution logs, and memory profiles.
A checklist for cache misses, oversized targets, broad globs, and missing visibility boundaries.
Recommended next measurements before changing remote execution settings.

output_examples.input: Ask for dependency graph commands for a changed library.
output: Bazel query commands for dependencies and reverse dependencies.
A graph-generation option for visual review.
A warning to inspect generated target sets before running broad builds.

best_practices: Pin external dependencies and verify checksums before relying on remote archives.
Start with measured profiling data before changing jobs, cache, or execution settings.
Use fine-grained targets and visibility rules to improve cache reuse and architecture boundaries.
anti_patterns: Copying placeholder remote cache or execution URLs into production configuration.
Using broad dependency globs when explicit target dependencies are practical.
Changing remote execution settings without checking toolchain and platform compatibility.

faq.question: Does this skill run Bazel commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides examples and templates that a user can review and run manually.

faq.question: Can it configure remote execution for my provider?
faq.answer: It explains the Bazel settings and platform concepts, but provider endpoints and credentials must come from your infrastructure.

faq.question: Is this only for TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: No. The examples cover TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, custom rules, and general Bazel performance patterns.

faq.question: Will it fix slow builds without profiling data?
faq.answer: It can suggest common improvements, but reliable optimization needs profile, query, or CI timing data.

faq.question: Are the remote URLs in the examples production ready?
faq.answer: No. They are placeholders and must be replaced with trusted internal services.

faq.question: What should I check before using the shell examples?
faq.answer: Check the target patterns, repository size, generated file lists, and output paths before running broad queries or builds.
